Implement the following plan:

# Plan: Revise all test files to match test_sinf.c quality

## Context

test_sinf.c is the reference template with 8 comprehensive test categories and ~400 test points. All other test files created in the previous session are missing key sections and have fewer test points.

Two classes of files need revision:

1. **Flat-style single-precision files** (test_cosf.c, test_expf.c, etc.) - compact style in `main()`, no named test functions, missing sNaN/-NaN, hard cases, exhaustive ULP sweep
2. **Structured double-precision files** (test_sin.c, test_exp.c, etc.) - have named functions but missing: sNaN/-NaN, mid-denorm, test_hard, test_exhaustive_small, sweep only 100 steps

Reference file: `/home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv/test/test_sinf.c`

## Required Structure (8 sections from test_sinf.c)

```c
test_special()      // zeros, qNaN, sNaN, -NaN, ±Inf
test_tiny()         // min/mid/max denorm ±, FLT/DBL_MIN ±, 1e-10 ±
test_known()        // function-specific mathematical constants
test_large()        // 10, 100, ..., 1e20, ±FLT/DBL_MAX
test_quadrant()     // boundary conditions (k * period ± epsilon)
test_hard()         // worst-case bit patterns for each function
test_sweep()        // 200 steps over main domain (both directions)
test_exhaustive_small() // ULP-level step over critical region
```

## NaN/special bit patterns

**Single precision:**
- qNaN: `0x7fc00000u`
- sNaN: `0x7f800001u`
- -NaN: `0xffc00000u`
- +Inf: `0x7f800000u`
- -Inf: `0xff800000u`
- min denorm: `0x00000001u` / `0x80000001u`
- mid denorm: `0x00400000u` / `0x80400000u`
- max denorm: `0x007fffffu` / `0x807fffffu`

**Double precision:**
- qNaN: `0x7ff8000000000000ULL`
- sNaN: `0x7ff0000000000001ULL`
- -NaN: `0xfff8000000000000ULL`
- +Inf: `0x7ff0000000000000ULL`
- -Inf: `0xfff0000000000000ULL`
- min denorm: `0x0000000000000001ULL` / `0x8000000000000001ULL`
- mid denorm: `0x0008000000000000ULL` / `0x8008000000000000ULL`
- max denorm: `0x000fffffffffffffULL` / `0x800fffffffffffffULL`

## File Groups and Function-Specific Test Content

### Group 1: Single-precision trig (restructure flat → 8-section)
- test_cosf.c, test_tanf.c
- test_asinf.c, test_acosf.c, test_atanf.c, test_atan2f.c

Hard cases for sin/cos/tan: pi/2pi bit patterns `0x40490fdbu`, `0x40c90fdbu`, `0x4096cbe4u`, powers 4/16/64/256
Exhaustive: [0, pi/4] every 4194304 ULP (same as sinf)
Sweep: [-4*pi, 4*pi] 200 steps

For asin/acos: domain [-1,1], hard cases near ±1.0, exhaustive [-1,1] every 65536 ULP
For atan: all reals, hard cases near inflection points, exhaustive [-pi/4, pi/4]
For atan2: quadrant boundaries with test_quadrant showing all 4 quadrants + axes

### Group 2: Single-precision exp/log (restructure flat → 8-section)
- test_expf.c, test_logf.c, test_log10f.c

exp hard cases: near overflow (`0x42d4e000u` ~ 105.375), underflow (`0xc2d40000u`), ln(2) bit pattern
exp exhaustive: [0, 1] every 4194304 ULP
log hard cases: near 1.0 (where log is small), powers of 2
log exhaustive: [1, 2] every 4194304 ULP (critical region)

### Group 3: Single-precision hyperbolic
- test_sinhf.c, test_coshf.c, test_tanhf.c, test_asinhf.c

Hard cases: overflow threshold ~88.7 for sinhf/coshf, tanh saturation near ±4
Exhaustive: [-1, 1] for tanh, [0, 1] for sinh

### Group 4: Single-precision misc
- test_sqrtf.c, test_cbrtf.c, test_powf.c
- test_ceilf.c, test_floorf.c
- test_frexpf.c, test_ldexpf.c, test_modff.c, test_fmodf.c
- test_fabsf.c, test_fpclassifyf.c

sqrt: Pythagorean values (3/4/5, 5/12/13), exhaustive [0, 4]
cbrt: cubes of small integers, exhaustive [0, 1]
ceil/floor: integer boundaries ±0.5, ±1.5, etc., exhaustive [0, 10]
fmod: various divisors, special cases

### Group 5: Double-precision trig
- test_sin.c, test_cos.c, test_tan.c
- test_asin.c, test_acos.c, test_atan.c

Add to existing structure:
- sNaN and -NaN to test_special
- mid denorm to test_tiny
- test_hard with pi bit patterns: `0x400921FB54442D18ULL`, `0x401921FB54442D18ULL`
- test_exhaustive_small: [0, pi/4] every 4503599627370496 ULP (2^52 / 1024)
- Increase sweep to 200 steps

### Group 6: Double-precision exp/log
- test_exp.c, test_log.c, test_log10.c, test_expm1.c, test_log1p.c

Add: sNaN/-NaN, subnormals, test_hard, test_exhaustive_small, sweep
exp hard: `0x40862E42FEFA39EULL` (ln(2)*128), near overflow 709, underflow -745
log hard: powers of 2 bit patterns, near 1.0
expm1: near 0 critical, test_hard with values near where expm1(x) ≈ x

### Group 7: Double-precision hyperbolic
- test_sinh.c, test_cosh.c, test_tanh.c

Add: sNaN/-NaN, test_hard, test_exhaustive_small

### Group 8: Double-precision rounding
- test_ceil.c, test_floor.c, test_trunc.c, test_round.c, test_rint.c
- test_lrint.c, test_lround.c
- test_fmod.c, test_remainder.c, test_remquo.c
- test_modf.c, test_frexp.c, test_ldexp.c
- test_ilogb.c, test_nextafter.c, test_fpclassify.c
- test_fmax.c, test_fmin.c, test_fabs.c

Rounding hard cases: 0.5 exactly, n+0.5 for various n, large integers
exhaustive: [0, 10] for rounding functions
ilogb: powers of 2 and odd multiples

### Group 9: Double-precision special
- test_sqrt.c, test_pow.c, test_hypot.c
- test_erf.c, test_erfc.c, test_lgamma.c
- test_acosh.c, test_atanh.c

### Group 10: Bessel functions
- test_j0.c, test_j1.c, test_jn.c, test_y0.c, test_y1.c, test_yn.c

Hard cases: zeros of each Bessel function (known values)
Large arguments: up to 100
Note: y-functions only defined for x > 0

## Execution Strategy

Revise files in parallel using 3 agents, each handling 2 groups at a time:
- Agent A: Groups 1+2 (sf_ trig, sf_ exp/log)
- Agent B: Groups 3+4 (sf_ hyperbolic, sf_ misc)
- Agent C: Groups 5+6 (double trig, double exp/log)
- Then: Groups 7+8, Groups 9+10

Each agent: read existing file, write complete replacement matching test_sinf.c structure.

## Critical Files

- Reference: `/home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv/test/test_sinf.c`
- All other test files in same directory

## Verification

After revision, spot-check a few files to confirm:
1. test_special has 7 entries (zeros, 3 NaN variants, 2 Inf)
2. test_tiny has 10+ entries including denorm variants
3. test_hard exists and has function-specific cases
4. test_exhaustive_small exists with ULP-level loop
5. test_sweep uses 200 steps


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /home/nick/.claude/projects/-home-nick-work-picolibc-agentic-libm-machine-riscv/957e0d38-6a19-4927-96c3-ef90ae307769.jsonl

file.content:
# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size           — report .text size for each assembly function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function

TOOLCHAIN = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
PREFIX    = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

ARCH    = -march=rv32imc -mabi=ilp32
CFLAGS  = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm

QEMU = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm

# Assembly sources
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Functions to test (must have test_<name>.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS = sinf cosf tanf atanf fabsf ceilf floorf expf sinhf coshf tanhf \
        logf log10f sqrtf asinf acosf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf modff \
        fpclassifyf

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),1)
  Q =
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  = @printf "  LD\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q = @
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
endif

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

# For each function, build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	@$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES,$(f))))

# --- Top-level targets ---

OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)

all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)

test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

size: $(ASM_OBJS)
	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS), \
		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
	@echo "  --------------------"
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')

list:
	@echo "Targets: all size test-all clean list"
	@echo "Per-function: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))"

clean:
	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt

.PHONY: all size test-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))

               fmax fmin finite ilogb nextafter fma llrint llround

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE)

# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size           — report .text size for each assembly function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function

TOOLCHAIN = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
PREFIX    = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

ARCH    = -march=rv32imc -mabi=ilp32
CFLAGS  = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm

QEMU = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm

# Assembly sources
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf fabsf ceilf floorf expf sinhf coshf tanhf \
               logf log10f sqrtf asinf acosf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf modff \
               fpclassifyf atan2f expm1f log1pf cbrtf asinhf tanhf coshf \
               erff erfcf lgammaf acoshf atanhf

# Double-precision functions (must have test_<name>.c; links against toolchain libm)
FUNCS_DOUBLE = sin cos tan atan atan2 fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh tanh \
               log log10 sqrt asin acos fmod pow frexp ldexp modf \
               fpclassify expm1 log1p cbrt asinh acosh atanh \
               erf erfc lgamma j0 j1 jn y0 y1 yn \
               hypot remainder remquo rint round lrint lround trunc \
               fmax fmin finite ilogb nextafter fma llrint llround

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE)

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),1)
  Q =
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_CC  = @printf "  CC\t%s\n" $@;
  MSG_LD  = @printf "  LD\t%s\n" $@;
else
  Q = @
  MSG_AS =
  MSG_CC =
  MSG_LD =
endif

# --- Pattern rules ---

%.o: %.s
	$(MSG_AS)$(AS) $(ASFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

$(TESTDIR)/%.o: $(TESTDIR)/%.c
	$(MSG_CC)$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c -o $@ $<

# For single-precision functions: build opt (with asm) and ref (toolchain only) ELFs.
# The asm object name: strip trailing 'f' from func name to get sf_<base>.o
# e.g. sinf -> sf_sin.o, log10f -> sf_log10.o, fpclassifyf -> sf_fpclassify.o
define FUNC_RULES_SINGLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o sf_$(patsubst %f,%,$(1)).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	@$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.elf > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt
	@if diff -q $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt > /dev/null 2>&1; then \
		printf "  %-14s PASS\n" $(1); \
	else \
		printf "  %-14s FAIL (%d diffs)\n" $(1) \
			$$$$(diff $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_opt.txt | grep -c '^[<>]'); \
	fi
endef

# For double-precision functions: build ref ELF only (no assembly optimization)
define FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE
$(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf: $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1).o
	$$(MSG_LD)$$(LD) -o $$@ $$^ $$(LDFLAGS)

test-$(1): $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf
	@$$(QEMU) $$(QEMU_FLAGS) $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.elf > $(TESTDIR)/test_$(1)_ref.txt
	@printf "  %-14s RUN\n" $(1)
endef

$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_SINGLE,$(f))))
$(foreach f,$(FUNCS_DOUBLE),$(eval $(call FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE,$(f))))

# --- Top-level targets ---

OPT_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS_SINGLE),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_opt.elf)
REF_ELFS = $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),$(TESTDIR)/test_$(f)_ref.elf)

all: $(OPT_ELFS) $(REF_ELFS)

test-all: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))
	@echo "Done."

size: $(ASM_OBJS)
	@echo "=== .text size (bytes) ==="
	@$(foreach o,$(ASM_OBJS), \
		printf "  %-20s %d\n" $(basename $(o)) \
			$$($(SIZE) -A $(o) | awk '/\.text/ {print $$2}');)
	@echo "  --------------------"
	@printf "  %-20s %d\n" "TOTAL" \
		$$($(SIZE) -A $(ASM_OBJS) | awk '/\.text/ {s+=$$2} END {print s}')

list:
	@echo "Targets: all size test-all clean list"
	@echo "Per-function: $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))"

clean:
	rm -f $(ASM_OBJS) $(TESTDIR)/*.o $(TESTDIR)/*.elf $(TESTDIR)/*_ref.txt $(TESTDIR)/*_opt.txt

.PHONY: all size test-all clean list $(foreach f,$(FUNCS),test-$(f))

# Makefile for libm accuracy test — A/B comparison
# Target: RV32IM soft-float (Andes optimized assembly)
#
# Usage:
#   make size           — report .text size for each assembly function
#   make test-all       — build, run opt vs ref in QEMU, diff outputs
#   make test-sinf      — test a single function

TOOLCHAIN = /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast542/build-toolchain/linux/nds32le-elf-mculib-v5
PREFIX    = $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv32-elf-

CC  = $(PREFIX)gcc
AS  = $(PREFIX)gcc
LD  = $(PREFIX)gcc

ARCH    = -march=rv32imc -mabi=ilp32
CFLAGS  = $(ARCH) -O2 -mvh -Wall
ASFLAGS = $(ARCH)
LDFLAGS = $(ARCH) -mvh -lm

QEMU = /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv32
QEMU_FLAGS = -cpu andes-n25 -nographic -M andes_ae350 -semihosting -bios

SIZE = $(PREFIX)size
NM   = $(PREFIX)nm

# Assembly sources
ASM_SRCS = sf_atan.s sf_ceil.s sf_cos.s sf_fabs.s sf_floor.s sf_frexp.s \
           sf_ldexp.s sf_modf.s sf_sin.s sf_tan.s sf_tanh.s \
           sf_acos.s sf_asin.s sf_cosh.s sf_exp.s sf_fmod.s sf_log.s \
           sf_log10.s sf_pow.s sf_sinh.s sf_sqrt.s \
           sf_fpclassify.s
ASM_OBJS = $(ASM_SRCS:.s=.o)

# Note: sf_sincos.c and sf_scalbn.c need picolibc headers, excluded from
# standalone size target. Measure those via full meson build.

# Single-precision functions (must have test_<name>f.c and sf_<name>.s)
FUNCS_SINGLE = sinf cosf tanf atanf asinf acosf fabsf ceilf floorf expf \
               sinhf coshf tanhf logf log10f sqrtf fmodf powf frexpf ldexpf \
               modff fpclassifyf atan2f asinhf cbrtf

# Double-precision functions (must have test_<name>.c; links against toolchain libm)
FUNCS_DOUBLE = sin cos tan atan asin acos fabs ceil floor exp sinh cosh tanh \
               log log10 sqrt fmod pow frexp ldexp modf fpclassify \
               acosh atanh expm1 log1p erf erfc lgamma \
               j0 j1 jn y0 y1 yn hypot remainder remquo rint round lrint \
               lround trunc fmax fmin ilogb nextafter

# All functions to test
FUNCS = $(FUNCS_SINGLE) $(FUNCS_DOUBLE)

# Test output directory
TESTDIR = test

# Verbose build output (set V=1 to see brief build progress)
V ?= 0
ifeq ($(V),1)
  Q =
  MSG_AS  = @printf "  AS\t%s\n" $@;

Makefile revised to test all 69 functions (25 single-precision + 44 double-precision):

**Changes:**
- Split FUNCS into FUNCS_SINGLE and FUNCS_DOUBLE
- Single-precision: sinf, cosf, tanf, atanf, asinf, acosf, fabsf, ceilf, floorf, expf, sinhf, coshf, tanhf, logf, log10f, sqrtf, fmodf, powf, frexpf, ldexpf, modff, fpclassifyf, atan2f, asinhf, cbrtf
- Double-precision: sin, cos, tan, atan, asin, acos, fabs, ceil, floor, exp, sinh, cosh, tanh, log, log10, sqrt, fmod, pow, frexp, ldexp, modf, fpclassify, acosh, atanh, expm1, log1p, erf, erfc, lgamma, j0, j1, jn, y0, y1, yn, hypot, remainder, remquo, rint, round, lrint, lround, trunc, fmax, fmin, ilogb, nextafter
- Separate test macros: FUNC_RULES_SINGLE (builds opt + ref, compares), FUNC_RULES_DOUBLE (builds ref only, runs)
- `make test-all` now runs all 69 function tests
- `make list` shows all available per-function targets
